subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Voyager's Grand Tour
description: A short lesson to help you apply Voyager's Grand Tour.

hook.text: In 1977, Voyager 1 and 2 launched to visit the giant planets. Both still send signals from interstellar space.

buildup.text: A rare alignment let one probe slingshot from planet to planet. Such alignment occurs once every 176 years.

discovery.text: Voyager found volcanoes on Io, geysers on Triton, and signs of a hidden ocean beneath Europa's ice.

twist.text: Each carries a Golden Record—music, greetings in 55 languages, and images of Earth for any finder.

climax.text: Voyager 1 entered interstellar space in 2012—the farthest human-made object, over 24 billion km away.

punchline.text: Launched in 1977, still calling home. The farthest hello.

quiz.question: What is special about the Golden Record carried by each Voyager probe?
options: It contains music, images, and greetings intended for any alien civilization that finds it
It stores scientific data collected during the mission
It plays a recording of mission control communications
